sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to gari destined for direct human consumption which is obtained from the processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz).
sections.retrieval_text:

sections.title: Definition of the product
sections.text: Gari is the finished product obtained by artisanal or industrial processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz). The processing consists of peeling, washing and grating of the tubers, followed by fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, drying if necessary, sifting and suitable heat treatment.i Gari is presented as flour of variable granule size.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Definition of the product
number=2.1
level=2
Gari is the finished product obtained by artisanal or industrial processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz). The processing consists of peeling, washing and grating of the tubers, followed by fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, drying if necessary, sifting and suitable heat treatment.i Gari is presented as flour of variable granule size.

page_spans.text: Gari is the finished product obtained by artisanal or industrial processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz). The processing consists of peeling, washing and grating of the tubers, followed by fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, drying if necessary, sifting and suitable heat treatment.i Gari is presented as flour of variable granule size.

sections.title: When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
path_titles: When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.retrieval_text:
HYGIENE > When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
number=5.3
level=2
– shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health;  
– shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and  
– shall not contain any substance originating from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

3 FAO and WHO. 1985. General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No.  CXS 1-1985. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
4 FAO and WHO. 1999. Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 234-1999. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.

1 FAO and WHO. 1995. General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 193-1995. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.  
2 FAO and WHO. 1969. General Principles of Food Hygiene. Codex Alimentarius Code of Practice, No. CXC 1-1969. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
3 FAO and WHO. 1985. General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No.  CXS 1-1985. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
4 FAO and WHO. 1999. Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 234-1999. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.

sentences.original: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, amendments were made in the Annex.

prefilter.reason: Contextual, specific to a regulatory decision.

classification.sentence: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, amendments were made in the Annex.
classification.pattern: Complex sentence stating an event and consequence

components.event: Decisions taken at Forty-sixth Session of Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023
components.consequence: Amendments were made in the Annex

classification.justification: The sentence provides an informative update about a procedural change; no regulatory modality or prescriptive function is expressed, and it doesn't match known regulatory templates.
notes: Purely informative without normative content

sentences.original: This standard applies to gari destined for direct human consumption which is obtained from the processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz).

prefilter.reason: Relevant to regulation of gari, an food product.

classification.sentence: This standard applies to gari destined for direct human consumption which is obtained from the processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz).
classification.pattern: Declarative sentence specifying applicable product and process

components.subject: This standard
components.applicable_product: gari destined for direct human consumption
components.product_source: processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz)

classification.justification: The sentence specifies scope conditions with a defining function; no known templates capture this exact scope statement structure.
notes: Common regulatory scope statement
Involves condition modality by specifying target product

sentences.original: Gari is the finished product obtained by artisanal or industrial processing of cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz).

prefilter.reason: Insufficient context, not a standalone regulation.

classification.sentence: is presented as flour of variable granule size.
classification.justification: Insufficient context, not a standalone regulation.

sentences.original: The processing consists of peeling, washing and grating of the tubers, followed by fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, drying if necessary, sifting and suitable heat treatment.i

sentences.original: According to good manufacturing practices, gari shall be practically free from extraneous matter.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: According to good manufacturing practices, gari shall be practically free from extraneous matter.
classification.pattern: Conditional phrase + obligation clause with shall + quality feature

components.obligation: shall be practically free from extraneous matter

classification.justification: Obligation formulated with "shall", specifying quality limit, but combined with a conditional introductory phrase; partial match to typical obligation templates.
notes: Structure includes a contextual condition phrase

sentences.original: The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).1

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General standard for contaminants and toxins in food and feed (CXS 193-1995).1
classification.pattern: Subject + 'shall comply with' + reference to standard limits

components.subject: The product covered by this standard
components.requirement: shall comply with the maximum levels of the General standard for contaminants and toxins in food and feed (CXS 193-1995)

classification.justification: Typical obligatory compliance statement referencing existing limits; partial template match as reference clause is explicit.
notes: Reference-based compliance obligation

sentences.original: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999)4 relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.

classification.justification: Obligation prescribing usage of specified methods for compliance checking; typical methodological obligation with reference.
notes: Compliance verification methodology obligation

sentences.original: TOTAL ACIDITY MIN: 0.6% determined as lactic acid – and – MAX: 1% determined as lactic acid Refer to Section 8 CRUDE FIBRE MAX: 2% Refer to Section 8 ASH MAX: 2.75% Refer to Section 8 ENRICHMENT vitamins proteins other nutrients Conform with legislation of the country in which the product is sold None defined FOOD ADDITIVES

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: None defined CLASSIFICATION Refer to Section 8 extra-fine gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 0.50 mm sieve – and – MIN: 40% by weight shall pass through a 0.25 mm sieve fine grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 1 mm sieve – and – MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through a 0.5 mm sieve medium grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 1.25 mm sieve – and – MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through 1.00 mm sieve coarse grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 2 mm sieve – and – MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through a 1.25 mm sieve unclassified gari Buyer preference
classification.function: Defines product classification based on sieve size passing limits
classification.pattern: Classification categories each with minimum and maximum quantitative thresholds and references

classifications: extra-fine gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 0.50 mm sieve and MIN: 40% by weight shall pass through a 0.25 mm sieve
fine grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 1 mm sieve and MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through a 0.5 mm sieve
medium grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 1.25 mm sieve and MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through 1.00 mm sieve
coarse grain gari MIN: 100% by weight shall pass through a 2 mm sieve and MAX: 40% by weight shall pass through a 1.25 mm sieve
unclassified gari Buyer preference

classification.justification: Classification with numeric limits and minimum/maximum thresholds partially matches known classification templates but with complex sieve specifics; adaptation indicated.
notes: Structured as classification with limits
Includes buyer preference category without numeric limits

pages.title: STANDARD FOR GARI
pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR GARI' e o cabeçalho 'CXS 151-1989'. Não há seções ou texto interno estruturado nesta página.
A página contém um cabeçalho 'CXS 151-1989 2' e uma seção de nível 1 intitulada '2023 Amendment' com texto descritivo. Não há título principal do documento visível nesta página.

Página mapeada com 6 seções principais (nível 1) numeradas de 1 a 6. Contém subtítulos até nível 3 com texto associado conforme a hierarquia numérica e títulos em caixa alta. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas. Cabeçalho identificado e texto interno limpo para RAG.

A página contém dois títulos principais numerados (7 e 8) com seus respectivos subtítulos para o item 7. O texto está corretamente fragmentado e associado a cada nó. O título 7 indica continuidade da página anterior, sinalizando que a seção já vinha antes desta página. O cabeçalho e numeração foram identificados, e texto repetitivo de cabeçalho foi excluído do conteúdo interno.

A página apresenta uma hierarquia com um título principal não numerado 'ANNEX' seguido de um agrupador 'Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis' contendo os detalhes dos fatores descritos na página. A continuação da seção 'ANNEX' da página anterior é marcada corretamente sem fragmentação adicional. O texto está associado conforme o conteúdo da página, sem incluir cabeçalhos repetitivos.

A página apresenta um título estruturado principal 'NOTES' em nível 1 com várias entradas de notas listadas em texto interno. Não há subtítulos nem continuidade evidenciada com outras páginas nesta página.
